content.content:
-- $Id: testes/attrib.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

print "testing require"

assert(require"string" == string)
assert(require"math" == math)
assert(require"table" == table)
assert(require"io" == io)
assert(require"os" == os)
assert(require"coroutine" == coroutine)

assert(type(package.path) == "string")
assert(type(package.cpath) == "string")
assert(type(package.loaded) == "table")
assert(type(package.preload) == "table")

assert(type(package.config) == "string")
print("package config: "..string.gsub(package.config, "\n", "|"))

do
  -- create a path with 'max' templates,
  -- each with 1-10 repetitions of '?'
  local max = _soft and 100 or 2000
  local t = {}
  for i = 1,max do t[i] = string.rep("?", i%10 + 1) end
  t[#t + 1] = ";"    -- empty template
  local path = table.concat(t, ";")
  -- use that path in a search
  local s, err = package.searchpath("xuxu", path)
  -- search fails; check that message has an occurrence of
  -- '??????????' with ? replaced by xuxu and at least 'max' lines
  assert(not s and
         string.find(err, string.rep("xuxu", 10)) and
         #string.gsub(err, "[^\n]", "") >= max)
  -- path with one very long template
  local path = string.rep("?", max)
  local s, err = package.searchpath("xuxu", path)
  assert(not s and string.find(err, string.rep('xuxu', max)))
end

do
  local oldpath = package.path
  package.path = {}
  local s, err = pcall(require, "no-such-file")
  assert(not s and string.find(err, "package.path"))
  package.path = oldpath
end


do  print"testing 'require' message"
  local oldpath = package.path
  local oldcpath = package.cpath

  package.path = "?.lua;?/?"
  package.cpath = "?.so;?/init"

  local st, msg = pcall(require, 'XXX')

  local expected = [[module 'XXX' not found:
	no field package.preload['XXX']
	no file 'XXX.lua'
	no file 'XXX/XXX'
	no file 'XXX.so'
	no file 'XXX/init']]

  assert(msg == expected)

  package.path = oldpath
  package.cpath = oldcpath
end

print('+')


-- The next tests for 'require' assume some specific directories and
-- libraries.

if not _port then --[

local dirsep = string.match(package.config, "^([^\n]+)\n")

-- auxiliary directory with C modules and temporary files
local DIR = "libs" .. dirsep

-- prepend DIR to a name and correct directory separators
local function D (x)
  local x = string.gsub(x, "/", dirsep)
  return DIR .. x
end

-- prepend DIR and pospend proper C lib. extension to a name
local function DC (x)
  local ext = (dirsep == '\\') and ".dll" or ".so"
  return D(x .. ext)
end


local function createfiles (files, preextras, posextras)
  for n,c in pairs(files) do

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
3	#
4	# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
5	# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
6	# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
7	# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
8	# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
9	# context.
10	#
11	# Usage:
12	#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua
13	
14	set -uo pipefail
15	
16	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
17	cd "$ROOT"
18	
19	TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
20	BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
21	OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
22	mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
23	
24	if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
25	    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
26	    exit 2
27	fi
28	
29	TEST_FILE="$1"
30	if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
31	    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
32	    exit 2
33	fi
34	
35	if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
36	    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
37	    exit 2
38	fi
39	
40	BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
41	COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
42	OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"
43	
44	PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
45	_soft = true
46	_port = true
47	_nomsg = true
48	_U = false
49	arg = arg or {}
50	_G = _G or _ENV
51	if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
52	'
53	
54	{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"
55	
56	TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
57	export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"
58	
59	run_with_timeout() {
60	    local src_file="$1"
61	    local out_file="$2"
62	    local src
63	    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
64	    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
65	        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
66	    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
67	        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
68	    else
69	        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
70	        local pid=$!
71	        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
72	        local watcher=$!
73	        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
74	        local rc=$?
75	        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
76	        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
77	        return $rc
78	    fi
79	}
80	
81	run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true
82	
83	reached_exec=0
84	grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1
85	
86	if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
87	    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
88	    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
89	    exit 0
90	fi
91	
92	if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
93	    cause="stub"
94	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
95	    cause="parse-panic"
96	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
97	    cause="codegen-panic"
98	elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
99	    cause="runtime-panic"
100	elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
101	    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
102	        cause="runtime-error"
103	    else
104	        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
105	    fi
106	else
107	    cause="unknown"
108	fi
109	
110	printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
111	echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
112	{
113	    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
114	        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
115	} | head -n 5
116	echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
117	exit 1
118

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

content.content:
1	//! Dynamic library loader for the Lua `package` library.
2	//!
3	//! Ported from `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/loadlib.c` (758 lines, ~25 functions).
4	//!
5	//! Provides `require`, `package.loadlib`, `package.searchpath`, and the four
6	//! built-in module searchers (preload, Lua-file, C-library, C-root).
7	//!
8	//! ## Platform-specific dynamic loading
9	//! The C source conditionally compiled one of three backends (POSIX dlfcn,
10	//! Windows LoadLibraryEx, or a fallback stub). All three `lsys_*` stubs here
11	//! return the "dynamic libraries not enabled" message because:
12	//! - The real implementations require `unsafe` (dlopen/GetProcAddress).
13	//! - `unsafe` is banned in `lua-stdlib` per PORTING.md §1.
14	//!
15	//! `readable()` also requires `std::fs`, which is banned outside `lua-cli`.
16	//!
17	//! Both issues are flagged with `TODO(port)` and must be resolved in Phase B
18	//! by either raising the unsafe budget or delegating to a capability interface.
19	
20	use std::env;
21	
22	use lua_types::{
23	    GcRef, LuaClosure, LuaError, LuaString, LuaType, LuaValue, StackIdx, LuaStatus,
24	};
25	use lua_types::value::LuaTable;
26	use crate::state_stub::{LuaState, LuaStateStubExt as _, lua_CFunction, upvalue_index, CompareOp, LuaDebug};
27	
28	// ── Module-level constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29	
30	// C: #define LUA_POF "luaopen_"
31	const LUA_POF: &[u8] = b"luaopen_";
32	
33	// C: #define LUA_OFSEP "_"
34	const LUA_OFSEP: &[u8] = b"_";
35	
36	// C: static const char *const CLIBS = "_CLIBS";
37	const CLIBS: &[u8] = b"_CLIBS";
38	
39	// C: #define LIB_FAIL "open"
40	const LIB_FAIL: &[u8] = b"open";
41	
42	// C: LUA_PATH_SEP — path-list separator
43	const LUA_PATH_SEP: u8 = b';';
44	
45	// C: LUA_PATH_MARK — wildcard character in path templates
46	const LUA_PATH_MARK: u8 = b'?';
47	
48	// C: LUA_IGMARK — ignore-mark in C module names
49	const LUA_IGMARK: u8 = b'-';
50	
51	// C: LUA_DIRSEP — directory separator (platform-specific)
52	#[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
53	const LUA_DIRSEP: u8 = b'\\';
54	#[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
55	const LUA_DIRSEP: u8 = b'/';
56	
57	// C: LUA_CSUBSEP / LUA_LSUBSEP — subseparators when mapping module names to paths
58	// Both default to LUA_DIRSEP on all platforms.
59	const LUA_CSUBSEP: u8 = LUA_DIRSEP;
60	const LUA_LSUBSEP: u8 = LUA_DIRSEP;

delta.partial_json: ", "old_string": "/// Find a module file using the path stored in `package[pname]`.\n///\n/// C:
`static const char *findfile(lua_State *L, const char *name,\n///                                  const char *pname, const
char *dirsep)`\n///\n/// TODO(port): Should return `Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, LuaError>` to
properly\n/// propagate the `\"'package.<pname>' must be a string\"` error. Currently returns\n/// `None` and loses the error, which will c
ause a confusing failure downstream.\nfn findfile(state: &mut LuaState, name: &[u8], pname: &[u8], dirsep: u8) -> Result
The package table is upvalue #1 for the searcher closures.\n    let uv = state.upvalue_index(1);\n
let _ = state.get_field(uv, pname);\n    // C: path = lua_tostring(L, -1);\n    let path_opt: Option<Vec<u8>> = state.to_b
ytes(-1);\n    let Some(path) = path_opt else {\n        // C: if (l_unlikely(path == NULL)) luaL_error(L, \"'package.%s' must be a string
\", pname);\n        // TODO(port): Cannot return Err here without changing the signature.\n        //             For now p
op the nil and return None (error is silently dropped).\n        state.pop_n(1);\n        return
", "new_string": "/// Find a module file using the path stored in `package[pname]`.\n///\n/// C: `static const char *findfile(lua_State *L, const char *
name,\n///                                  const char *pname, const char *dirsep)`\nfn findfile(state: &mut LuaState, name: &[u8], pname: &[u8], dirsep: u8) ->

delta.text:
green (only pre-existing warnings, no errors).

**Root cause:** `findfile` in `crates/lua-st
A `TODO(port)` that swallowed the `'package.path' must be a string` error when `package.path` was set
to a non-string value (e.g. a table). It popped the nil and returned `Ok(None)`, which let `
searcher_lua` continue and produced a generic "module not found" message that did not contain the substring `"package.path"`
— failing the assertion at attrib.lua line 46 (`assert(

not s and string.find(err, "package.path"))`).

**Fix:** Replaced the silent `Ok(None)` with
`Err(LuaError::runtime("'package.<pname>' must be a string"))`, matching the C `luaL_error` in `
loadlib.c findfile`. The signature was already `Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, LuaError>`, so the stale comment was wrong — no

signature change was needed.

**State after fix:** Compile is green (`cargo build -q -p
lua-cli` produces no errors, only pre-existing warnings about unused `Result`s elsewhere). I did not re-exec
ute attrib.lua per the instructions — the mega-loop will re-test. The next time `pcall(require, "
no-such-file")` runs with `package.path = {}`, `err` will contain the string `'package.path' must be a string`, which
contains `"package.path"`, so the assertion should now pass.

Build is green (only pre-existing warnings, no errors).

**Root cause:** `findfile` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/loadlib.rs:613` had a leftover Phase-A `TODO(port)` that swallowed the `'package.path' must be a string` error when `package.path` was set to a non-string value (e.g. a table). It popped the nil and returned `Ok(None)`, which let `searcher_lua` continue and produced a generic "module not found" message that did not contain the substring `"package.path"` — failing the assertion at attrib.lua line 46 (`assert(not s and string.find(err, "package.path"))`).

**Fix:** Replaced the silent `Ok(None)` with `Err(LuaError::runtime("'package.<pname>' must be a string"))`, matching the C `luaL_error` in `loadlib.c findfile`. The signature was already `Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, LuaError>`, so the stale comment was wrong — no signature change was needed.

**State after fix:** Compile is green (`cargo build -q -p lua-cli` produces no errors, only pre-existing warnings about unused `Result`s elsewhere). I did not re-execute attrib.lua per the instructions — the mega-loop will re-test. The next time `pcall(require, "no-such-file")` runs with `package.path = {}`, `err` will contain the string `'package.path' must be a string`, which contains `"package.path"`, so the assertion should now pass.

result:
Build is green (only pre-existing warnings, no errors).

**Root cause:** `findfile` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/loadlib.rs:613` had a leftover Phase-A `TODO(port)` that swallowed the `'package.path' must be a string` error when `package.path` was set to a non-string value (e.g. a table). It popped the nil and returned `Ok(None)`, which let `searcher_lua` continue and produced a generic "module not found" message that did not contain the substring `"package.path"` — failing the assertion at attrib.lua line 46 (`assert(not s and string.find(err, "package.path"))`).

**Fix:** Replaced the silent `Ok(None)` with `Err(LuaError::runtime("'package.<pname>' must be a string"))`, matching the C `luaL_error` in `loadlib.c findfile`. The signature was already `Result<Option<Vec<u8>>, LuaError>`, so the stale comment was wrong — no signature change was needed.

**State after fix:** Compile is green (`cargo build -q -p lua-cli` produces no errors, only pre-existing warnings about unused `Result`s elsewhere). I did not re-execute attrib.lua per the instructions — the mega-loop will re-test. The next time `pcall(require, "no-such-file")` runs with `package.path = {}`, `err` will contain the string `'package.path' must be a string`, which contains `"package.path"`, so the assertion should now pass.
